audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Shema, covenant love, teaching, remembrance, and child-question discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Shema, love with heart/soul/might, words on heart, teaching children, binding/doorpost signs, prosperity and forgetting, exclusive service, Massah warning, and Exodus catechesis.

verses.restored: This is command, statutes, and judgments YHWH your Divine commanded to teach you, to do in land where you are crossing to possess it,
so you may fear/revere YHWH your Divine, keeping all his statutes and commands that I command you—you, your child, and your child's child—all days of your life, and so your days may lengthen.
Hear, Israel, and keep to do, so it may be well with you and you may multiply greatly, as YHWH Divine of your fathers spoke to you, in land flowing with milk and honey.
Hear, Israel: YHWH is our Divine, YHWH is one.
You shall love YHWH your Divine with all your heart, with all your soul/life, and with all your might/veryness.
These words that I command you today shall be upon your heart.
You shall repeat/teach them diligently to your children and speak of them when you sit in your house, when you walk on way, when you lie down, and when you rise.
You shall bind them as sign upon your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.
You shall write them upon doorposts of your house and upon your gates.
It shall be when YHWH your Divine brings you into land he swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give you—great and good cities you did not build,
houses full of every good thing you did not fill, hewn cisterns you did not hew, vineyards and olive trees you did not plant—and you eat and are satisfied,
guard yourself lest you forget YHWH who brought you out from land of Egypt, from house of slaves.
YHWH your Divine you shall fear/revere, him you shall serve, and by his name you shall swear.
You shall not go after other gods from gods of peoples around you,
for a jealous Divine is YHWH your Divine in your midst, lest anger of YHWH your Divine burn against you and destroy you from face of ground.
You shall not test YHWH your Divine as you tested at Massah.
You shall diligently keep commands of YHWH your Divine and his testimonies and statutes that he commanded you.
You shall do right and good in eyes of YHWH, so it may be well with you and you may enter and possess good land YHWH swore to your fathers,
to thrust out all your enemies from before you, as YHWH spoke.
When your child asks you tomorrow, saying, What are testimonies and statutes and judgments YHWH our Divine commanded you?
you shall say to your child: We were slaves to Pharaoh in Egypt, and YHWH brought us out from Egypt with strong hand.
YHWH gave signs and wonders, great and harmful, against Egypt, Pharaoh, and all his house before our eyes.
He brought us out from there in order to bring us in, to give us land he swore to our fathers.
YHWH commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear/revere YHWH our Divine, for our good all days, to keep us alive, as this day.
It shall be righteousness for us when we keep to do all this command before YHWH our Divine, as he commanded us.

translation_notes: The Shema—Deuteronomy 6:4-9—is central living Jewish prayer and covenant practice. It must not be detached from Judaism, Christianized, or treated as superseded.
Echad may express one, unique, or unified; the restored line preserves the traditional compact ambiguity.
Heart, soul/life, and might/veryness describe total embodied devotion, not emotional coercion or erasure of self.
Teaching occurs through ordinary daily rhythms and later Jewish practices of tefillin and mezuzah deserve explicit respect.

The warning about enjoying cities, houses, cisterns, vineyards, and olive trees not created by Israel exposes the danger of forgetting both liberation and the labor/displacement behind inherited abundance.
The child's answer begins with slavery and liberation, making Exodus the interpretive center of commandment.
safety_note: Do not appropriate the Shema against Jewish people, use love of Divine to coerce emotion or suppress identity, or weaponize this chapter for conquest. Center Jewish continuity, daily teaching, gratitude, remembrance of slavery, and responsibility within inherited abundance.
